meta.certificationName: AWS Certified Generative AI Developer – Professional
meta.title: AWS-GAP-2: Bedrock Orchestration, Safety, and Operations

questions.stem: Which capabilities are provided by Amazon Bedrock Guardrails? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Guardrails can detect and block or mask sensitive information in prompts and model responses.
Guardrails supports denied topics policies to block categories of requests or responses that your application does not allow.
Cross-Region replication for prompts is not a Guardrails capability.
Warehouse autoscaling is unrelated to Amazon Bedrock.

questions.stem: A chatbot gives different answers for the same question after a model upgrade. Which action should the team do first?

options.text: Run regression evaluation on the old and new model outputs
options.explanation: Comparing old and new outputs on a fixed benchmark is the fastest way to identify whether the upgrade changed quality or safety.
Lambda memory may affect app runtime, not model quality drift.
Deleting the knowledge base removes evidence and functionality.

options.text: Move the application to another Region
options.explanation: Region changes do not explain the regression.

questions.stem: A team wants a chat application that can maintain a single request/response interaction with an Amazon Bedrock model using the newer conversational API. Which API is the best fit?

options.explanation: `Converse` is the Bedrock API designed for conversational interactions and supports messages plus inference configuration in a consistent request shape.
Streaming can be useful, but it is not the primary newer conversational API abstraction.
Async invocation is for long-running asynchronous inference jobs, not ordinary chat turns.
Model customization jobs are for tuning, not inference.

questions.stem: A developer is designing a global application and discovers the preferred model is not available in one Region. Which actions are reasonable next steps? (Select TWO)

options.text: Check Bedrock model availability and features by Region
options.explanation: Bedrock model availability and supported features vary by Region, so the first step is to verify the Regional support matrix.

options.text: Evaluate another supported model that meets requirements
options.explanation: If the preferred model is unavailable, the team should evaluate another supported model that still meets functional, latency, and compliance requirements.

options.text: Assume all models are globally identical and continue
options.explanation: Bedrock models are not globally identical in availability or feature support.

options.text: Move the entire application to AWS Outposts to unlock model access
options.explanation: Moving an app to Outposts is not the normal solution for Bedrock model availability.

questions.stem: A team wants AWS-managed workflows for comparing model outputs with automatic metrics and optional human review. Which Bedrock capability should they use?

options.explanation: Bedrock model evaluation supports automated and human-based evaluation workflows for comparing model quality and safety.
Cross-Region inference affects routing, not evaluation.
Knowledge bases support retrieval, not model comparison workflows.
Prompt caching optimizes reuse, not quality measurement.

questions.stem: Which metric destination would a team typically use to monitor Bedrock-powered application behavior over time on AWS?

options.explanation: CloudWatch is the standard AWS service for application metrics, logs, and alarms.
Resolver handles DNS resolution.
Artifact distributes compliance reports.
Organizations manages accounts.

questions.stem: Which Bedrock feature is specifically intended to help build retrieval-augmented generation applications with managed data connectors and retrieval?

options.explanation: Knowledge Bases are the managed Bedrock feature for RAG-style retrieval from approved content.
Guardrails moderate content.
Provisioned throughput reserves capacity.
StackSets deploy infrastructure, not RAG.

questions.stem: Which controls help reduce the risk of exposing sensitive data in prompts or outputs? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Guardrails can filter or mask sensitive information in prompts and outputs.
Least-privilege IAM reduces the chance that connected services or operators can expose sensitive prompts or responses.

options.text: Publicly shared debug logs with full prompts
options.explanation: Public debug logs with full prompts increase data-exposure risk.
Broad wildcard permissions violate least-privilege guidance.

questions.stem: A call center app receives steady, predictable traffic during business hours and strict p95 latency targets. Why would Provisioned Throughput be preferred over only on-demand invocations?

options.text: It reserves inference capacity for more predictable performance
options.explanation: Provisioned Throughput is intended for reserved inference capacity and more consistent performance under predictable demand.
It does not perform data labeling.

options.text: It eliminates the need for IAM policies
options.explanation: IAM is still required.
Observability remains necessary.

questions.stem: A delivery team needs to process a large backlog of documents with Bedrock without blocking interactive users. Which pattern is most appropriate?

options.explanation: Batch inference is the fit for large asynchronous processing workloads rather than interactive request/response usage.
Browser loops are inefficient and interactive.
Word filters do not process documents.
Parameter Store stores configuration only.

questions.stem: A team wants an agent to book travel by consulting external systems and then summarizing the result for the user. What is the core reason to use Bedrock Agents instead of only direct model invocation?

options.text: Agents can orchestrate tool use through defined action groups
options.explanation: Agents add orchestration for multi-step tasks and tool use, which is not provided by a plain one-shot model call.

options.text: Agents always cost less than any direct invocation
options.explanation: Cost depends on workload and design.

options.text: Agents remove the need for IAM roles
options.explanation: IAM is still required.
Agents do not automatically fine-tune models.

questions.stem: A developer wants Bedrock to answer from approved enterprise documents stored in Amazon S3 with managed retrieval. Which feature should be implemented?

options.explanation: Knowledge Bases for Bedrock provide managed ingestion, embeddings, storage integration, and retrieval for RAG patterns.
Provisioned throughput addresses capacity, not document retrieval.
Inference profiles route model invocations, not document retrieval pipelines.
Workflows can orchestrate steps but do not provide managed RAG by themselves.

questions.stem: Which feature best matches the requirement to answer questions over a curated document corpus with managed retrieval and citations?

options.explanation: Knowledge Bases are the managed Bedrock feature for retrieval over curated enterprise data.
Throughput concerns capacity.
Guardrails concern moderation.
Budgets concerns spend tracking.

questions.stem: An agent must invoke an order-status API that requires custom business logic, authentication checks, and response normalization. Which implementation is most appropriate?

options.text: Back the action group with AWS Lambda
options.explanation: Lambda provides the controlled execution layer for Bedrock agent tools, including authentication, transformation, and downstream API calls.

options.text: Put the API URL inside the user prompt
options.explanation: Prompt text alone does not create a secure callable tool.

options.text: Train a custom foundation model to memorize order data
options.explanation: Memorizing operational data in a model is unsafe and inflexible.
Event notifications do not execute agent tools.

questions.stem: An application needs one-off text generation against a specific model without maintaining a chat message structure. Which Bedrock API should the developer choose?

options.explanation: `InvokeModel` is the core inference API for sending a request directly to a foundation model.
Guardrails moderate content but do not replace model inference.
Retrieve-and-generate is for RAG workflows, not a basic direct prompt.
Prompt router is a separate routing capability, not the base inference call.

questions.stem: A product owner asks for human preference ratings between two prompt strategies. Which Bedrock capability addresses this need most directly?

options.explanation: Bedrock model evaluation supports human-based evaluation workflows for comparative assessment.
Caching affects efficiency, not ratings.
Word filters moderate content but do not gather preference judgments.
Access Analyzer reviews permissions.

questions.stem: Before releasing a prompt update, which checks are most valuable? (Select TWO)

options.text: Run a benchmark set that represents production tasks
options.explanation: A benchmark set that mirrors production tasks helps detect quality regressions before release.

options.text: Review safety outcomes on adversarial prompts
options.explanation: Adversarial or safety-focused prompts help reveal new unsafe behaviors introduced by the prompt update.

options.text: Assume unchanged behavior because the model is the same
options.explanation: Prompt changes can materially change outputs even when the underlying model stays the same.

options.text: Skip evaluation if unit tests pass
options.explanation: Unit tests alone do not measure answer quality or safety.

questions.stem: Which activity belongs most clearly in the testing and validation domain for a GenAI system?

options.text: Measuring answer quality on representative prompts
options.explanation: Testing and validation focus on measuring the behavior and quality of model-driven outputs.

options.text: Changing the VPC DHCP option set
options.explanation: DHCP settings are networking administration.
Storage classes are data lifecycle decisions.
Hosted zones are DNS configuration.

questions.stem: A workload sends the same long system prompt and policy context on nearly every request. Which Bedrock capability can reduce repeated token processing cost and latency?

options.explanation: Prompt caching is designed to reuse repeated prompt context so the application does not pay the full cost of reprocessing unchanged content every time.
Customization changes model behavior but does not solve repeated prompt overhead per request.
Denied topics are safety controls, not performance optimizations.
Batch inference is for asynchronous large-scale jobs, not repeated interactive prompts.

questions.stem: A developer needs token-by-token responses in a chat UI. Which Bedrock approach best supports this?

options.explanation: `ConverseStream` streams generated content incrementally for conversational applications.
Knowledge base creation is administrative setup, not inference streaming.
Availability lookup does not return generated tokens.
Listing guardrails is unrelated to response streaming.

questions.stem: A security team is reviewing a GenAI application on AWS. Which practices best align with responsible AI and data protection guidance? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use least-privilege IAM permissions for Bedrock integrations
options.explanation: Least-privilege IAM is a core control for Bedrock integrations and connected AWS resources.

options.text: Evaluate prompts and outputs for harmful content
options.explanation: Reviewing prompts and outputs for harmful or unsafe content aligns with responsible AI and data-protection guidance.

options.text: Grant broad administrator permissions to simplify experimentation
options.explanation: Broad administrator permissions increase security risk.

options.text: Store sensitive prompts in public read-only S3 buckets
options.explanation: Sensitive prompts should not be stored in public S3 buckets.

questions.stem: Which statement about prompt caching is most accurate?

options.text: It is most useful when substantial prompt context repeats across requests
options.explanation: Prompt caching is valuable when repeated context would otherwise be reprocessed on many requests.
Evaluation measures quality.

options.text: It is required before using Guardrails
options.explanation: Guardrails do not depend on caching.

options.text: It stores vectors for every knowledge base automatically
options.explanation: Knowledge-base vector storage is a separate concern.

questions.stem: A team wants a single application layer that can swap among supported Bedrock models while keeping a common conversational request shape. Which API best enables that portability?

options.explanation: `Converse` normalizes conversational requests across supported models, improving application portability.
Customization jobs do not define the runtime request interface.
S3 Select queries objects, not models.
AWS Batch is unrelated.

questions.stem: A prompt change appears to lower answer quality for common support questions. Which is the best way to detect that regression before broad release?

options.text: Run evaluation against a representative benchmark set
options.explanation: Regression testing with representative prompts and expected outcomes is the direct way to measure whether quality declined.
Temperature changes behavior but does not measure regression.
Disabling logs removes useful evidence.

options.text: Rotate the KMS key for the application
options.explanation: KMS rotation is good hygiene but not a quality test.

questions.stem: Which statements are true about foundation model selection in Amazon Bedrock? (Select TWO)

options.text: Different models support different modalities and use cases
options.explanation: Bedrock models differ in supported modalities, capabilities, and best-fit use cases.

options.text: Latency and pricing can vary by model
options.explanation: Pricing and latency vary by model, so they are important selection criteria.

options.text: All Bedrock models behave identically for every task
options.explanation: Models from different providers do not behave identically on every task.

options.text: Every model is available in every Region by default
options.explanation: Model availability differs by Region.

questions.stem: Which statement about Guardrails is correct?

options.text: They help enforce safety policies across model interactions
options.explanation: Guardrails are the Bedrock control plane for safety policies such as content filtering and sensitive-information handling.

options.text: They train a custom model from scratch
options.explanation: Training a custom model is a different capability.
Redshift provisioning is unrelated.
Authentication is still an application and IAM concern.

questions.stem: A developer needs to know whether a Bedrock model supports streaming responses and tool use before coding. Where should they check first?

options.explanation: The model features documentation lists supported capabilities by model, including inference-related features.
Trusted Advisor does not document model features.
Bucket policy is unrelated.
Route tables are unrelated.

questions.stem: An organization must block topics such as medical advice and redact PII before model output is returned to end users. Which Bedrock feature should be configured?

options.explanation: Bedrock Guardrails support denied topics, content filters, and sensitive information filtering such as PII redaction.
CloudWatch alarms observe metrics but do not moderate responses.
Macie classifies S3 data, not live model prompts and responses.
Config checks resource compliance, not model output safety.

questions.stem: A team is designing a RAG application and wants to reduce hallucinations. Which actions are most aligned with AWS guidance? (Select TWO)

options.text: Ground responses in retrieved source content
options.explanation: Grounding answers in retrieved source content helps reduce unsupported responses.

options.text: Evaluate outputs with domain-specific test sets
options.explanation: Domain-specific test sets are useful for evaluating whether the RAG system stays accurate and well grounded.

options.text: Increase temperature to maximize creativity for all prompts
options.explanation: Increasing temperature usually increases variability and can worsen factual consistency.

options.text: Disable citations to avoid exposing sources
options.explanation: Hiding citations removes a useful way for users to inspect grounding.

questions.stem: A production chatbot has highly predictable traffic and strict latency SLOs. Which Bedrock option is most appropriate?

options.explanation: Provisioned Throughput reserves model inference capacity to provide more predictable throughput and latency characteristics for steady workloads.
Lambda might host app code, but it does not reserve model capacity.
Transfer Acceleration is unrelated to model inference.
Glue streaming jobs are not a Bedrock capacity feature.

questions.stem: A developer is selecting a foundation model for a new workload. Which factors are most important to evaluate first? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Acceptable latency and cost are key model-selection constraints.

options.text: Whether the model supports the required modalities
options.explanation: The model must support the modalities required by the workload, such as text, image, or multimodal inputs.

options.text: The default VPC CIDR block for the account
options.explanation: The account's VPC CIDR block does not determine model suitability.

options.text: The Region where Amazon S3 was first used
options.explanation: Historical S3 usage in a Region is unrelated to choosing a foundation model.

questions.stem: Which Bedrock feature is explicitly designed for comparing model quality with automated and human evaluation methods?

options.explanation: Model evaluation is the Bedrock feature for quality comparison and assessment.
Prompt caching is a latency/cost feature.
Action groups expose tools.
Provisioned Throughput reserves capacity.

questions.stem: An organization wants to screen user prompts for disallowed topics before any model response is generated. Which feature should be enabled?

options.explanation: Guardrails can inspect prompts and enforce denied topic policies before or during inference handling.
Object Lock is for WORM storage.
Vault Lock protects backup retention policies.
Snapshot restore is unrelated.

questions.stem: Which capabilities are commonly associated with Amazon Bedrock Agents? (Select TWO)

options.text: Reasoning over user intent to decide tool use
options.explanation: Bedrock Agents interpret requests and decide when tools should be used.
Action groups are the Bedrock Agents mechanism for invoking configured external operations.

options.text: Provisioning an Amazon Redshift cluster automatically
options.explanation: Provisioning an Amazon Redshift cluster is unrelated to standard agent capabilities.
Agents do not replace every workflow engine in an application architecture.

questions.stem: A RAG application returns an answer that is fluent but unsupported by retrieved documents. What problem is this?

options.explanation: A fluent answer that is not supported by retrieved evidence indicates poor grounding and a hallucination risk.
Caching concerns reuse, not factual support.
Throughput reservations do not affect grounding by themselves.
CloudWatch sampling is irrelevant.

questions.stem: A team is evaluating a RAG assistant. Which metrics are most useful for this evaluation? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Faithfulness measures whether an answer stays supported by the retrieved evidence.
Context relevance measures whether the retrieved material is actually useful for the question being answered.
VPC subnet utilization is infrastructure telemetry, not a RAG quality metric.
S3 multipart upload count does not measure retrieval or answer quality.

questions.stem: Which AWS control is most directly designed to prevent unsafe model outputs from being returned to users in real time?

options.explanation: Guardrails are applied during inference to screen prompts and responses according to configured policies.
Lifecycle rules govern object retention.
Auto Scaling manages compute fleets, not model output policy.
Athena workgroups govern SQL usage.

questions.stem: An organization wants an agent to call multiple business tools based on user intent. Which action should the developer configure?

options.explanation: Agents can be extended with multiple action groups so they can invoke different tools for different tasks.
Bucket versioning does not define agent tools.
Endpoint policies govern access, not intent routing.
Health checks are unrelated.

questions.stem: A team wants dashboards and alarms for latency and operational errors in its Bedrock-powered application. Which AWS service should they use?

options.explanation: CloudWatch is the primary AWS observability service for metrics, logs, alarms, and dashboards.
ACM manages TLS certificates.
Cognito manages identities.
Budgets tracks spend, not full operational telemetry.

questions.stem: A delivery team needs to let an Amazon Bedrock agent call a private business API. What is the recommended Bedrock pattern for this integration?

options.text: Create an action group backed by AWS Lambda
options.explanation: Bedrock Agents use action groups, and Lambda is the standard way to implement tool execution logic for agent actions.

options.text: Mount the API server directly inside the foundation model
options.explanation: Models cannot directly host arbitrary private APIs.

options.text: Attach an Amazon S3 bucket as the only tool
options.explanation: S3 stores content but is not a general action execution mechanism.

options.text: Use a CloudFormation stack as a conversational tool at runtime
options.explanation: CloudFormation is not a Bedrock agent runtime tool interface.

questions.stem: A team is building an enterprise assistant with Bedrock Agents. Which components are commonly part of the design? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Action groups let Bedrock Agents call tools and business APIs.

options.text: A knowledge source for retrieval when needed
options.explanation: A knowledge source is commonly paired with an agent when retrieval over approved content is required.

options.text: EBS Multi-Attach volumes for prompt memory
options.explanation: EBS Multi-Attach volumes are unrelated to Bedrock agent design.
Route tables do not classify user intent.

questions.stem: Which actions can reduce inference cost for a production GenAI application on AWS? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: A smaller suitable model often reduces per-request inference cost.

options.text: Reuse repeated context with prompt caching
options.explanation: Prompt caching can reduce repeated processing cost when the same context is reused.

options.text: Increase output token limits for all requests
options.explanation: Higher output-token limits usually increase cost.

options.text: Duplicate every request across two models by default
options.explanation: Duplicating every request across two models roughly doubles spend.

questions.stem: A developer needs to stream generated content to the client as it is produced. Which invocation style should be preferred?

options.text: A streaming Bedrock API such as `ConverseStream`
options.explanation: Streaming inference APIs are designed to deliver partial outputs as generation occurs.
Glue jobs are batch ETL.
Inventory reports list objects.
DataSync transfers files.

questions.stem: Which statements about Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: They can use Amazon S3 as a data source
options.explanation: Knowledge Bases can use Amazon S3 as a supported data source.

options.text: They are intended for managed RAG retrieval workflows
options.explanation: Knowledge Bases are intended for managed retrieval-augmented generation workflows.

options.text: They require the developer to manually implement every embedding call
options.explanation: Bedrock can manage key embedding and retrieval plumbing for supported Knowledge Base patterns.

options.text: They replace IAM authorization for Bedrock access
options.explanation: IAM remains required for authorization and access control.

questions.stem: Which choices improve operational efficiency for a stable high-volume GenAI app? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use Provisioned Throughput for predictable capacity
options.explanation: Provisioned Throughput helps stable high-volume workloads with predictable capacity and performance.

options.text: Monitor latency and error metrics in CloudWatch
options.explanation: CloudWatch metrics and alarms improve operational visibility and help teams manage SLAs efficiently.

options.text: Increase maximum output tokens without need
options.explanation: Unnecessarily larger output limits increase cost and latency.

options.text: Bypass all retrieval steps regardless of task
options.explanation: Retrieval may be necessary for grounded answers, so bypassing it by default does not improve operational efficiency.
